返回日报
Hacker News #9

所以 Reddit 认为纯 HTML 是不安全的

So Reddit has decided that plain HTML is unsafe

AI 作者 montroser 热度 568 回复 584 2026/07/22 20:32
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • Reddit-The-Company If you don’t know Reddit, it basically is the host of many popular forums. And like any company which encourages you to “come for the cats [and] stay for the empathy,” Reddit seems to be in the business of extracting as much value as it can from said forums without completely destroying them. After all, simply fostering community is not a noble enough goal for the New Tech, and fortunately for Reddit, genuinely human-generated data is now gold in the LLM Age. You are welcome to read about the last time they decided to pluck the metaphorical liver from their communities. Reddit-The-Search-Results While I no longer wish to engage with Reddit, I still visit it occasionally, especially in the LLM Age. This is because appending site: reddit.com to a search query is basically a surefire way to find results written by genuine humans. Which, just to be extremely clear, I still find desirable. Now behind a login… sort of After doing such a query yesterday, to my absolute delight I was greeted with I guess this makes me old, but I use the original frontend for Reddit, old.reddit.com. I’m going to try really hard not to preach about why it’s a better frontend, but that’s all it is! A design for Reddit. Look, I know I’m a fringe user. I use Firefox. I noscript! I am no stranger to being forced off a product that worked just fine because someone decides to no longer support the two people who still use it.
  • It happened to my phone of 8 years, which works fine by the way, but is on too outdated of an OS. May it rest in peace in its tiny glory.

命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

tdeck 2026/07/23 19:53
我一直想知道 Reddit 如何证明 old.reddit 效应的合理性。如果您的新设计非常糟糕，以至于很大一部分用户拒绝使用它，而更喜欢十多年前的（性能更高！）版本，那么这难道不是一个强烈的信号，表明您犯了一些错误吗？为什么不尝试通过调查和解决导致人们使用旧版本的问题来使新版本更具吸引力。

我想这些年来为保持 old.reddit 运行而进行内部斗争的人都值得赞扬。我确信它已经多次成为被砍掉的候选人。

I always wondered how Reddit justified the old.reddit effect. If your new design is so awful that a large percentage of users refuse to use it, and prefer the (more performant!) version from more than a decade ago, shouldn't that be a strong signal that you've made some mistakes? Why not try making the new version more appealing by investigating and fixing the issues that cause people to use the old one.

I guess credit to whoever fought internally to keep old.reddit running all these years. I'm sure it's been a candidate for the axe many times.

楼层
zacharycohn 2026/07/23 20:54
我为工作修改了一个相当受欢迎的 Reddit 子版块，作为一名顽固的老 Reddit 用户查看统计数据，我对我们的人数实在太少感到震惊。
I mod a reasonably popular subreddit for work, and, looking at the stats as a die hard old reddit user, I was floored at how few of us there really are.
楼层
OGWhales 2026/07/23 23:13
这是将 old.reddit 与 reddit.com 进行比较吗？我使用的是正常的网址，但仍然使用旧的设计。我只是在首选项中切换了一个设置以使用旧设计，而无需转到 old.reddit，我使用 old.reddit 的唯一原因是我是否已注销。不过，这可能更小众，似乎大多数人实际上都使用其他网址，当有切换时，这总是让我感到困惑。
Is this comparing old.reddit to reddit.com? I am using the normal url but still use the old design. I just toggled a setting in preferences to use the old design without needing to go to old.reddit, only reason I ever use old.reddit is if I'm logged out. That might be even more niche though, it seems like most people actually use the other url which always confused me when there is a toggle.
楼层
dredmorbius 2026/07/23 23:21
从报告的统计数据来看，这一点尚不清楚。 但根据我自己的经验，从几年前开始（几年前我关闭了我的潜艇），“旧”只占总使用量的一小部分。 尽管事实上我积极促进了它在我的子项目中的使用（我想要并希望大大提高实用性和可读性的网站设计）。
That's not clear, from the reported stats. But from my own experience, as of several years ago (I closed my subs some years back), "old" was a small fraction of total usage. This despite the fact that I actively promoted its use for my subs (site design which I intended, and hope, greatly increased utility and readability).
楼层
al_borland 2026/07/23 21:09
大多数人接受给予他们的东西，并且不会努力改变它。

还有关于帐户年龄的统计数据吗？我想知道 Reddit 是否认为旧的 Reddit 用户问题可以通过自然流失来解决，因为人们会自然离开，新用户只知道新界面才加入该网站。

Most people accept what is given to them and won’t go through the effort to change it.

Are there any stats on account age as well? I wonder if Reddit figured the old Reddit user problem would be solved through attrition as people naturally left and new users join the site only knowing the new interface.

楼层
jazzypants 2026/07/23 21:16
我的意思是，我使用旧设计有几年了，但现在我真的更喜欢新设计。

我主要在手机上查看 Reddit，而新的 Reddit 对移动设备更加友好。我必须在旧版 reddit 上进行捏合、缩放和滚动。

我知道这些部分的观点不受欢迎，但我也讨厌在手机上查看这个网站，所以我使用了一个应用程序。这个网站确实需要重新设计移动设备，但我无法想象用户会抛出什么样的问题。

I mean, I used the old design for a couple years, but now I genuinely prefer the new design.

I mostly view Reddit on my phone, and new reddit is much more mobile friendly. I have to pinch and zoom and scroll all over the place on old reddit.

I know that's an unpopular opinion around these parts, but I also hate viewing this website on my phone so I use an app. This website really needs a mobile redesign, but I can't imagine the shit fit that the users would throw.

楼层
OGWhales 2026/07/23 23:18
他们曾经提供一个更合理的移动网站，但他们放弃了它。现在使用旧的设计只是桌面版本，由于您所说的原因，效果不佳。
They used to offer a more reasonable mobile website but they got rid of it. Using the old design now is just the desktop version which doesn't work well for the reasons you said.
楼层
danisth 2026/07/23 21:20
我发现了一个名为 SinkItforReddit 的 Safari 扩展，它可以很好地让旧版 Reddit 在移动设备上可用。我仍然增加了一些点击，但我很顽固，拒绝使用新网站。我对它的主要抱怨不是用户界面，而是内容排序感觉更加模糊，而旧网站向我展示了相当基本的内容排序启发式算法。
I found an extension for Safari called SinkItforReddit and it does an ok job of making old Reddit usable on mobile. I still fatfinger some clicks, but I’m stubborn and refuse to use the new site. My main gripe with it is less about the UI and more that the content ordering feels more obscured, whereas old site shows me the fairly basic heuristic algorithm for content order.
楼层
ksec 2026/07/23 22:46
还要补充一点，新的设计现在在 React 上经过了至少 5 - 6 年的迭代，终于可用并且更好了。

就像所有像样的 JS 前端/基于 React 的网站一样，需要数年的时间来打磨才能使其达到一定的标准。我不完全确定为什么我们必须这样做以及它实际上带来了什么好处？

Also want to add, the new design is now after at least 5 - 6 years of iterations on React, is finally useable and better.

Much like all decent JS front end / React based Web site, it takes years to polish to make it work to certain standards. I am not entirely sure why we have to do that and what benefits does it actually brings?

楼层
darepublic 2026/07/24 01:07
他们把一堆东西放在shadow dom中。 当然，许多决定不是基于可用性，而是基于货币化激励。 如果当前的决策者可以的话，他们会让 Reddit 成为像 Facebook 一样的围墙花园，但这种转变有点尴尬，因为它曾经是一个免费和开放的网站。看起来他们即将切断这种开放性。
they put a bunch of their stuff in shadow dom. A lot of the decisions of course are not based on usability but monetization incentives. If the current decision makers could, they would have made reddit a walled garden like facebook but the transition is a little awkward seeing as it used to be a free and open site. looks like they are close to cutting the cord on that openness.
楼层
jazzypants 2026/07/23 23:48
只是澄清一下：我认为旧版本可能使用了 React，但我只是仔细检查了一下，当前的 Reddit 绝对是一个使用 Web 组件构建的 Lit 应用程序。您可以根据渲染引擎用作锚节点的注释占位符以及标记中虚线元素的普遍程度来判断。下面是一个示例：

 
          
          发送 GIF
由于某种原因，他们似乎在内部将当前架构称为“shreddit”。
Just to clarify: I think an older version may have used React, but I just double-checked and current Reddit is definitely a Lit app built with web components. You can tell based on the comment placeholders used by the rendering engine as anchor nodes and by the prevalence of dashed-elements in the markup. Here's an example:

  <rpl-tooltip placement="top" appearance="neutral">
          <!--?lit$003985561$-->
          <span slot="content"><!--?lit$003985561$-->Send a GIF</span>
  </rpl-tooltip>
They seem to call the current architecture "shreddit" internally for some reason.
楼层
Melatonic 2026/07/24 01:42
可能“嘘”不要告诉任何人这个版本有多糟糕
Probably "shhh" don't tell anyone how bad this version this is
楼层
bix6 2026/07/23 20:58
请提供数字
Numbers please
楼层
Eisenstein 2026/07/23 21:18
以下是来自 10 万会员 subreddit 的统计数据，每天有 15,000 名独立访问者。

* https://imgur.com/UTJyyl8

Here are stats from from a 100k member subreddit with 15k unique visitors a day.

楼层
asddubs 2026/07/23 20:09
虽然我确实使用 old.reddit 并且真诚地认为它在 UI 方面更好，但在一般情况下，用户往往会抵制任何 UI 更改，因为它会破坏他们的习惯和肌肉记忆。这是有效的，但即使新设计更容易使用/以某种方式更好也适用。
While I do use old.reddit and genuinely think it's better UI-wise, in the general case users tend to be resistant to any UI change because it breaks their habits and muscle memory. This is valid but applies even if the new design is easier to use/better in one way or another.
楼层
Gormo 2026/07/23 20:35
> 虽然我确实使用 old.reddit 并且真诚地认为它在 UI 方面更好，但一般情况下，用户往往会抵制任何 UI 更改，因为它会破坏他们的习惯和肌肉记忆。

有趣的是，人们经常说“这只是习惯/肌肉记忆/等等”。回应这些类型的讨论，就好像它在某种程度上是一个反论点，而不是证实反对突然、剧烈的 UI 更改的论点。

人们很常见地回答“Z 因为 Y 而不起作用”，并认为他们在某种程度上击败了反对 Z 的论点，这真的很奇怪。如果你承认 X 存在并导致 Y，并接受 Y与 Z 不兼容，那么你就承认 Z 不成立！

> While I do use old.reddit and genuinely think it's better UI-wise, in the general case users tend to be resistant to any UI change because it breaks their habits and muscle memory.

It's funny how often people say "it's just habit / muscle memory / etc." in response to these kinds of discussions, as though it were somehow a counter-argument, and not something that corroborates the argument against making sudden, drastic UI changes.

It's really weird how common it is for people to say "well, Y is only true because of X" in response to "Z doesn't work because of Y", and thinking they're somehow defeating the argument against Z. If you acknowledge that X exists and causes Y, and accept that Y isn't compatible with Z, then you are conceding that Z does not hold!

楼层
throwaway27448 2026/07/23 21:18
我不认为这是一个有说服力的论据，而是认为质量并不重要（在这种情况下，以及在许多渐进式的enshitification情况下）
I don't see it as a persuasive argument so much as resignation that quality doesn't matter (in this case, and in many cases of incremental enshitification)
楼层
tdeck 2026/07/23 20:32
我并不是 Reddit 的普通用户，但每次打开 Google 结果时都会提醒我使用 old.reddit，并且无法快速浏览或搜索评论。在法学硕士之前，这个问题已经存在多年了，所以我不确定造成如此糟糕体验的理由是什么。
I'm not really a regular user of Reddit, but I'm reminded to use old.reddit every time I open a Google result and can't quickly skim or search the comments. That was an issue for years before LLMs so I'm not sure what the justification is for such a poor experience.
楼层
ruszki 2026/07/23 21:56
除了在这种情况下，它是在不点击的情况下看到完整的线程与点击多次来实现相同的事情。

此外，混乱......

这些是可衡量的事情，而不是喜怒无常的事情。

Except in this case, it’s seeing complete threads without clicking vs clicking many times to achieve the same thing.

Also, clutter…

These are measurable things, not moody things.

楼层
vovavili 2026/07/23 20:06
Reddit 的重新设计让我觉得这是一个方便的借口，可以让寻求 IPO 的公司强制无限滚动。
Reddit redesign strikes me as a convenient excuse to enforce infinite scrolling by a company looking to IPO.
楼层
